## reference · visual design principles
* Use the principle of scale to signal importance and rank in a composition, with no more than 3 different sizes.
* Apply the principle of visual hierarchy to guide the eye on the page, using variations in scale, value, color, spacing, and placement.
* Create a clear visual hierarchy using 2-3 typeface sizes to indicate importance.
* Use balance to create a satisfying arrangement or proportion of design elements, with symmetrical, asymmetrical, or radial balance.
* Apply the principle of contrast to convey differences between elements, using color, size, or other visual properties.
* Ensure sufficient contrast between text and its background to maintain legibility and accessibility.
* Use Gestalt principles, such as similarity, proximity, and closure, to organize complex images and create intuitive interfaces.
* Limit the number of design elements and use white space effectively to create a clear and simple visual design.
* Use bright colors for important items and muted colors for less important ones to create visual hierarchy.
* Avoid reducing text contrast to deemphasize less important text, as it can reduce legibility and accessibility.
Sources: https://www.nngroup.com/articles/principles-visual-design/
